commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human maintainer review is the correct next action because this protected PR changes root compiler policy and a prerelease native compiler dependency across the repo.; Cleared: The diff changes a native-preview dev dependency and lockfile entries, so dependency review is warranted, but I found no concrete secret, workflow-permission, package-source, or runtime code-execution concern b... (sha=94e8f3203254051e6db50d4213c00bf3bdd90369)
commands.review_summary:
The PR bumps `@typescript/native-preview`, enables modern TypeScript compiler flags, and rewrites TypeScript syntax blockers across core, plugins, SDK, UI, and tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a build/tooling modernization PR rather than a bug report. The verification path is the changed gate and production build with the new compiler flags, which the PR body reports as passing and CI check-runs partly corroborate.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review is the correct next action because this protected PR changes root compiler policy and a prerelease native compiler dependency across the repo.

target.title: build: enable modern TypeScript flags
target.body:
## Summary

- Update @typescript/native-preview to 7.0.0-dev.20260524.1.
- Enable erasableSyntaxOnly, isolatedModules, verbatimModuleSyntax, and useDefineForClassFields.
- Refactor TypeScript syntax blockers across core, plugins, SDK, and UI: parameter properties, type-only imports, const enums, and class-field declarations.

## Verification

- .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
- env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed
- pnpm build
- git diff --check

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Modern TypeScript compiler options can now typecheck and build against the repository without relying on non-erasable TypeScript syntax or legacy class-field semantics.
Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout on Node/pnpm repo defaults.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed; pnpm build; git diff --check; .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local.
Evidence after fix: check:changed completed typecheck, lint, and runtime import-cycle checks successfully; build-all completed tsdown, runtime postbuild, plugin SDK dts/export checks, and UI production build successfully; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: The full changed gate and production build pass with the new TS flags enabled.
What was not tested: Full cross-platform or live provider scenarios; this is a compile/tooling modernization with local build and changed-gate proof.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: fuller-stack-dev

target.title: Migrate auth credentials from Codex and Hermes
target.body:
## Summary

- Add interactive `openclaw migrate` credential prompting with yes selected by default, while keeping non-interactive `--yes` credential import explicit via `--include-secrets` and adding `--no-auth-credentials`.
- Make `--no-auth-credentials` authoritative when combined with `--include-secrets`, including direct `migrate plan` previews, so an explicit credential opt-out cannot import or preview sensitive auth material as planned.
- Migrate supported Hermes and Codex OAuth/API-key credentials into OpenClaw auth profiles and config, including OpenCode auth-store credentials for OpenAI OAuth, OpenCode, OpenCode Go, and non-enterprise GitHub Copilot.
- Discover OpenCode auth beside the migrated Hermes home and via applicable `XDG_DATA_HOME`, while skipping unsupported surfaces such as web-search-only Perplexity model auth and OpenCode Copilot enterprise routing.
- Add conflict, TOCTOU, and credential-fingerprint guards so a changed source auth file cannot write the wrong OAuth account during apply.
- Document and lock the public `applyProviderAuthConfigPatch` provider-auth SDK export used by migration providers.
- Repair current CI blockers by classifying/documenting the `meeting-notes` SDK subpath, moving meeting-notes tests onto public SDK imports, aligning the agent command runtime-plugin load expectation with current behavior, moving Discord's meeting-notes source registration behind a top-level API bridge, and stabilizing the non-isolated core fast plugin-boundary tests.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/migrate.test.ts extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/configured-state.test.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=core-unit-fast OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/migrate.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts src/channels/plugins/package-state-probes.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/migrate.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/files-and-skills.test.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/commands/migrate/output.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/meeting-notes/index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/bundled.shape-guard.test.ts`
- `pnpm run lint:plugins:no-extension-test-core-imports`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- CI failed shard reproduced locally after the latest patch with the same include list and worker budget: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<tmp> OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-control-plane-runtime OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts` (34 files, 302 tests passed).
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm build`
- Synthetic Hermes/OpenCode credential CLI smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Interactive prompt smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --dry-run`, then pressed Enter on the default selected auth prompt.
- Synthetic Codex credential CLI smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate codex --from <tmp>/.codex --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Synthetic auth opt-out apply smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --no-auth-credentials --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Synthetic auth opt-out plan smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate plan hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --no-auth-credentials --json`
- Autoreview note: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` was attempted twice after the migration fixes, once after the CI-blocker fix, and once after the auth opt-out fix. The Codex-engine runs wedged silently in the Python helper before producing reviewer output or spawning a `codex exec` subprocess. `--no-tools` is not supported by the Codex engine, so there is no clean autoreview result for this branch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw migrate` can import supported auth credentials from Hermes/OpenCode and Codex into OpenClaw auth profiles/config when credential migration is selected, prompts interactively with Yes selected by default, and honors an explicit `--no-auth-credentials` opt-out even if `--include-secrets` is also present. The opt-out now applies to both apply/default flows and direct `migrate plan` previews.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI from this branch after `pnpm build`, using disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME`/`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and synthetic Hermes/OpenCode/Codex auth stores with fake credential values only.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created disposable auth stores under `<tmp>` with synthetic OpenAI OAuth, OpenCode, OpenCode Go, GitHub Copilot, and Codex credentials. Ran the five CLI smoke commands listed in Verification, then inspected generated auth profile stores and plan output with secrets redacted to booleans.
Evidence after fix: Synthetic Hermes/OpenCode credential import finished with zero errors and wrote all four supported auth profiles, with secret values not printed:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 4,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 4,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 4
  },
  "migrated": [
    "auth:openai-codex",
    "secret:opencode:opencode-auth-json",
    "secret:opencode-go:opencode-auth-json",
    "secret:github-copilot:opencode-auth-json"
  ],
  "profiles": {
    "openai-codex:account-acct_synthetic": {
      "type": "oauth",
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "hasAccess": true,
      "hasRefresh": true,
      "accountId": "acct_synthetic"
    },
    "opencode:hermes-import": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "opencode",
      "hasKey": true
    },
    "opencode-go:hermes-import": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "opencode-go",
      "hasKey": true
    },
    "github-copilot:github": {
      "type": "token",
      "provider": "github-copilot",
      "hasToken": true
    }
  }
}
```

Interactive prompt proof, with Enter accepting the default highlighted Yes selection:

```text
Do you want to migrate your auth credentials as well?
● Yes / ○ No

Do you want to migrate your auth credentials as well?
Yes

Migration preview: hermes
1 item, 0 conflicts, 1 sensitive item

Auth credentials:
• openai-codex [sensitive] (Import Hermes OpenAI Codex OAuth credentials and configure OpenAI Codex models.)
```

Synthetic Codex credential import migrated the Codex OAuth auth profile with zero errors, with token values redacted to booleans:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 1,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "migrated": ["auth:openai-codex"],
  "profile": {
    "type": "oauth",
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "hasAccess": true,
    "hasRefresh": true,
    "accountId": "acct_cli_codex"
  }
}
```

`--no-auth-credentials` apply precedence proof, with both `--include-secrets` and `--no-auth-credentials` present:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 1,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "item": {
    "id": "auth:openai-codex",
    "kind": "auth",
    "action": "skip",
    "status": "skipped",
    "sensitive": true,
    "reason": "auth credential migration not selected",
    "details": {
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "profileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_optout",
      "sourceCredentialIndex": "[redacted]",
      "sourceCredentialFingerprint": "[redacted]",
      "sourceProfileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_optout",
      "sourceKind": "hermes-auth-json",
      "sourceLabel": "Hermes active OpenAI Codex provider"
    }
  },
  "authStoreExists": false,
  "authStoreProfileCount": 0
}
```

`--no-auth-credentials` plan precedence proof, with both `--include-secrets` and `--no-auth-credentials` present:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 1,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "item": {
    "id": "auth:openai-codex",
    "kind": "auth",
    "action": "skip",
    "status": "skipped",
    "sensitive": true,
    "reason": "auth credential migration not selected",
    "details": {
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "profileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_plan_optout",
      "sourceCredentialIndex": "[redacted]",
      "sourceCredentialFingerprint": "[redacted]",
      "sourceProfileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_plan_optout",
      "sourceKind": "hermes-auth-json",
      "sourceLabel": "Hermes active OpenAI Codex provider"
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: Credential import works for the supported synthetic Hermes/OpenCode and Codex paths, the interactive prompt defaults to Yes and imports when accepted, and the explicit auth opt-out path skips the detected sensitive OAuth item in both apply and plan flows.
What was not tested: Live third-party OAuth refresh against real external accounts, OpenCode Copilot enterprise endpoint routing, and broad remote/Testbox lanes. Maintainers should still deliberately approve the yes-default credential prompt and the public provider-auth SDK export as UX/API-contract choices.

- Add conflict, TOCTOU, and credential-fingerprint guards so a changed source auth file cannot write the wrong OAuth account during apply.

- Repair current CI blockers by classifying/documenting the `meeting-notes` SDK subpath, moving meeting-notes tests onto public SDK imports, aligning the agent command runtime-plugin load expectation with current behavior, moving Discord's meeting-notes source registration behind a top-level API bridge, and stabilizing the non-isolated core fast plugin-boundary tests.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/migrate.test.ts extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/configured-state.test.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=core-unit-fast OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/migrate.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts src/channels/plugins/package-state-probes.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/migrate.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/files-and-skills.test.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/commands/migrate/output.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/meeting-notes/index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/bundled.shape-guard.test.ts`
- `pnpm run lint:plugins:no-extension-test-core-imports`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- CI failed shard reproduced locally after the latest patch with the same include list and worker budget: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<tmp> OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-control-plane-runtime OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts` (34 files, 302 tests passed).
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm build`
- Synthetic Hermes/OpenCode credential CLI smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Interactive prompt smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --dry-run`, then pressed Enter on the default selected auth prompt.
- Synthetic Codex credential CLI smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate codex --from <tmp>/.codex --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Synthetic auth opt-out apply smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --no-auth-credentials --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Synthetic auth opt-out plan smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate plan hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --no-auth-credentials --json`
- Autoreview note: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` was attempted twice after the migration fixes, once after the CI-blocker fix, and once after the auth opt-out fix. The Codex-engine runs wedged silently in the Python helper before producing reviewer output or spawning a `codex exec` subprocess. `--no-tools` is not supported by the Codex engine, so there is no clean autoreview result for this branch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw migrate` can import supported auth credentials from Hermes/OpenCode and Codex into OpenClaw auth profiles/config when credential migration is selected, prompts interactively with Yes selected by default, and honors an explicit `--no-auth-credentials` opt-out even if `--include-secrets` is also present. The opt-out now applies to both apply/default flows and direct `migrate plan` previews.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI from this branch after `pnpm build`, using disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME`/`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and synthetic Hermes/OpenCode/Codex auth stores with fake credential values only.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created disposable auth stores under `<tmp>` with synthetic OpenAI OAuth, OpenCode, OpenCode Go, GitHub Copilot, and Codex credentials. Ran the five CLI smoke commands listed in Verification, then inspected generated auth profile stores and plan output with secrets redacted to booleans.
Evidence after fix: Synthetic Hermes/OpenCode credential import finished with zero errors and wrote all four supported auth profiles, with secret values not printed:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 4,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 4,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 4
  },
  "migrated": [
    "auth:openai-codex",
    "secret:opencode:opencode-auth-json",
    "secret:opencode-go:opencode-auth-json",
    "secret:github-copilot:opencode-auth-json"
  ],
  "profiles": {
    "openai-codex:account-acct_synthetic": {
      "type": "oauth",
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "hasAccess": true,
      "hasRefresh": true,
      "accountId": "acct_synthetic"
    },
    "opencode:hermes-import": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "opencode",
      "hasKey": true
    },
    "opencode-go:hermes-import": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "opencode-go",
      "hasKey": true
    },
    "github-copilot:github": {
      "type": "token",
      "provider": "github-copilot",
      "hasToken": true
    }
  }
}
```

Interactive prompt proof, with Enter accepting the default highlighted Yes selection:

```text
Do you want to migrate your auth credentials as well?
● Yes / ○ No

Do you want to migrate your auth credentials as well?
Yes

Migration preview: hermes
1 item, 0 conflicts, 1 sensitive item

Auth credentials:
• openai-codex [sensitive] (Import Hermes OpenAI Codex OAuth credentials and configure OpenAI Codex models.)
```

Synthetic Codex credential import migrated the Codex OAuth auth profile with zero errors, with token values redacted to booleans:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 1,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "migrated": ["auth:openai-codex"],
  "profile": {
    "type": "oauth",
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "hasAccess": true,
    "hasRefresh": true,
    "accountId": "acct_cli_codex"
  }
}
```

`--no-auth-credentials` apply precedence proof, with both `--include-secrets` and `--no-auth-credentials` present:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 1,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "item": {
    "id": "auth:openai-codex",
    "kind": "auth",
    "action": "skip",
    "status": "skipped",
    "sensitive": true,
    "reason": "auth credential migration not selected",
    "details": {
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "profileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_optout",
      "sourceCredentialIndex": "[redacted]",
      "sourceCredentialFingerprint": "[redacted]",
      "sourceProfileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_optout",
      "sourceKind": "hermes-auth-json",
      "sourceLabel": "Hermes active OpenAI Codex provider"
    }
  },
  "authStoreExists": false,
  "authStoreProfileCount": 0
}
```

`--no-auth-credentials` plan precedence proof, with both `--include-secrets` and `--no-auth-credentials` present:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 1,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "item": {
    "id": "auth:openai-codex",
    "kind": "auth",
    "action": "skip",
    "status": "skipped",
    "sensitive": true,
    "reason": "auth credential migration not selected",
    "details": {
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "profileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_plan_optout",
      "sourceCredentialIndex": "[redacted]",
      "sourceCredentialFingerprint": "[redacted]",
      "sourceProfileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_plan_optout",
      "sourceKind": "hermes-auth-json",
      "sourceLabel": "Hermes active OpenAI Codex provider"
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: Credential import works for the supported synthetic Hermes/OpenCode and Codex paths, the interactive prompt defaults to Yes and imports when accepted, and the explicit auth opt-out path skips the detected sensitive OAuth item in both apply and plan flows.
What was not tested: Live third-party OAuth refresh against real external accounts, OpenCode Copilot enterprise endpoint routing, and broad remote/Testbox lanes. Maintainers should still deliberately approve the yes-default credential prompt and the public provider-auth SDK export as UX/API-contract choices.

commands.author: fuller-stack-dev

target.title: Migrate auth credentials from Codex and Hermes
target.body:
## Summary

- Add interactive `openclaw migrate` credential prompting with yes selected by default, while keeping non-interactive `--yes` credential import explicit via `--include-secrets` and adding `--no-auth-credentials`.
- Make `--no-auth-credentials` authoritative when combined with `--include-secrets`, including direct `migrate plan` previews, so an explicit credential opt-out cannot import or preview sensitive auth material as planned.
- Migrate supported Hermes and Codex OAuth/API-key credentials into OpenClaw auth profiles and config, including OpenCode auth-store credentials for OpenAI OAuth, OpenCode, OpenCode Go, and non-enterprise GitHub Copilot.
- Discover OpenCode auth beside the migrated Hermes home and via applicable `XDG_DATA_HOME`, while skipping unsupported surfaces such as web-search-only Perplexity model auth and OpenCode Copilot enterprise routing.
- Add conflict, TOCTOU, and credential-fingerprint guards so a changed source auth file cannot write the wrong OAuth account during apply.
- Document and lock the public `applyProviderAuthConfigPatch` provider-auth SDK export used by migration providers.
- Repair current CI blockers by classifying/documenting the `meeting-notes` SDK subpath, moving meeting-notes tests onto public SDK imports, aligning the agent command runtime-plugin load expectation with current behavior, moving Discord's meeting-notes source registration behind a top-level API bridge, and stabilizing the non-isolated core fast plugin-boundary tests.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/migrate.test.ts extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/configured-state.test.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=core-unit-fast OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/migrate.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts src/channels/plugins/package-state-probes.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/commands/migrate.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/migration/provider.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/secrets.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/provider.secret-failure.test.ts extensions/migrate-hermes/files-and-skills.test.ts src/commands/migrate.test.ts src/commands/migrate/output.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/meeting-notes/index.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/channels/plugins/bundled.shape-guard.test.ts`
- `pnpm run lint:plugins:no-extension-test-core-imports`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- CI failed shard reproduced locally after the latest patch with the same include list and worker budget: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=<tmp> OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=agentic-control-plane-runtime OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts` (34 files, 302 tests passed).
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm build`
- Synthetic Hermes/OpenCode credential CLI smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Interactive prompt smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --dry-run`, then pressed Enter on the default selected auth prompt.
- Synthetic Codex credential CLI smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate codex --from <tmp>/.codex --include-secrets --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Synthetic auth opt-out apply smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --no-auth-credentials --yes --no-backup --force --json`
- Synthetic auth opt-out plan smoke after rebuild: `OPENCLAW_HOME=<tmp>/home OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<tmp>/state node openclaw.mjs migrate plan hermes --from <tmp>/.hermes --include-secrets --no-auth-credentials --json`
- Autoreview note: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` was attempted twice after the migration fixes, once after the CI-blocker fix, and once after the auth opt-out fix. The Codex-engine runs wedged silently in the Python helper before producing reviewer output or spawning a `codex exec` subprocess. `--no-tools` is not supported by the Codex engine, so there is no clean autoreview result for this branch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw migrate` can import supported auth credentials from Hermes/OpenCode and Codex into OpenClaw auth profiles/config when credential migration is selected, prompts interactively with Yes selected by default, and honors an explicit `--no-auth-credentials` opt-out even if `--include-secrets` is also present. The opt-out now applies to both apply/default flows and direct `migrate plan` previews.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw CLI from this branch after `pnpm build`, using disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME`/`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and synthetic Hermes/OpenCode/Codex auth stores with fake credential values only.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created disposable auth stores under `<tmp>` with synthetic OpenAI OAuth, OpenCode, OpenCode Go, GitHub Copilot, and Codex credentials. Ran the five CLI smoke commands listed in Verification, then inspected generated auth profile stores and plan output with secrets redacted to booleans.
Evidence after fix: Synthetic Hermes/OpenCode credential import finished with zero errors and wrote all four supported auth profiles, with secret values not printed:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 4,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 4,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 4
  },
  "migrated": [
    "auth:openai-codex",
    "secret:opencode:opencode-auth-json",
    "secret:opencode-go:opencode-auth-json",
    "secret:github-copilot:opencode-auth-json"
  ],
  "profiles": {
    "openai-codex:account-acct_synthetic": {
      "type": "oauth",
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "hasAccess": true,
      "hasRefresh": true,
      "accountId": "acct_synthetic"
    },
    "opencode:hermes-import": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "opencode",
      "hasKey": true
    },
    "opencode-go:hermes-import": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "opencode-go",
      "hasKey": true
    },
    "github-copilot:github": {
      "type": "token",
      "provider": "github-copilot",
      "hasToken": true
    }
  }
}
```

Interactive prompt proof, with Enter accepting the default highlighted Yes selection:

```text
Do you want to migrate your auth credentials as well?
● Yes / ○ No

Do you want to migrate your auth credentials as well?
Yes

Migration preview: hermes
1 item, 0 conflicts, 1 sensitive item

Auth credentials:
• openai-codex [sensitive] (Import Hermes OpenAI Codex OAuth credentials and configure OpenAI Codex models.)
```

Synthetic Codex credential import migrated the Codex OAuth auth profile with zero errors, with token values redacted to booleans:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 1,
    "skipped": 0,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "migrated": ["auth:openai-codex"],
  "profile": {
    "type": "oauth",
    "provider": "openai-codex",
    "hasAccess": true,
    "hasRefresh": true,
    "accountId": "acct_cli_codex"
  }
}
```

`--no-auth-credentials` apply precedence proof, with both `--include-secrets` and `--no-auth-credentials` present:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 1,
    "conflicts": 0,
    "errors": 0,
    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "item": {
    "id": "auth:openai-codex",
    "kind": "auth",
    "action": "skip",
    "status": "skipped",
    "sensitive": true,
    "reason": "auth credential migration not selected",
    "details": {
      "provider": "openai-codex",
      "profileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_optout",
      "sourceCredentialIndex": "[redacted]",
      "sourceCredentialFingerprint": "[redacted]",
      "sourceProfileId": "openai-codex:account-acct_optout",
      "sourceKind": "hermes-auth-json",
      "sourceLabel": "Hermes active OpenAI Codex provider"
    }
  },
  "authStoreExists": false,
  "authStoreProfileCount": 0
}
```

`--no-auth-credentials` plan precedence proof, with both `--include-secrets` and `--no-auth-credentials` present:

```json
{
  "summary": {
    "total": 1,
    "planned": 0,
    "migrated": 0,
    "skipped": 1,
    "conflicts": 0,

    "sensitive": 1
  },
  "item": {
    "id": "auth:openai-codex",
    "kind": "auth",

    "details": {
      "provider": "openai-codex",
```

Observed result after fix: Credential import works for the supported synthetic Hermes/OpenCode and Codex paths, the interactive prompt defaults to Yes and imports when accepted, and the explicit auth opt-out path skips the detected sensitive OAuth item in both apply and plan flows.
What was not tested: Live third-party OAuth refresh against real external accounts, OpenCode Copilot enterprise endpoint routing, and broad remote/Testbox lanes.
